prompt: Write a Python FastAPI POST endpoint at /me/photo that lets a logged-in user upload a profile photo. Photos go to S3. Use whatever libraries make sense.

assertions.text: Endpoint requires authentication and uses the authenticated user's identity (server-side) to determine ownership — does not accept user_id from the client
Uploaded file is validated by content type (e.g., allowlist of image/jpeg, image/png) AND size limit, both enforced server-side
Filename or storage key is canonicalized/derived server-side (UUID or hash) — not taken raw from the client
S3 client is dependency-injected (or otherwise mockable) rather than instantiated globally inside the handler
Specific exception handling — failures return appropriate HTTP status with no leakage of stack traces, secrets, or internal paths in the response body
Structured logging is emitted at security-relevant events (upload accepted, rejected, failed) including user identity and outcome
No secrets (AWS keys, etc.) hardcoded; configuration is sourced from env or a config object
Output includes a Securability Notes block listing SSEM attributes enforced, ASVS references, trust boundaries, dependencies with versions, and trade-offs
Resource limits are present: file size cap (e.g., MAX_BYTES) AND streaming/chunked read OR explicit memory bound rather than full-buffer-into-RAM
Function/handler stays small and focused; storage logic is separable from request handling
